text:
There once was a humble crow named Olga. Olga loved sunrays.

One bright afternoon, Olga spotted an old man sitting alone on a bench. Olga thought he looked lonely.

Feeling generous, Olga flew down from her perch tree.

Olga landed softly next to the old man.

“Poor thing. I wish I could fly.”

Olola blinked.

“That’s unusual,” said Olga. “Why do you think?”

“Oh…I guess I’m just old.” Olova thought.

“You think?”

“I think life isnlodgy…but I prefer birds.

Olga pondered.

“I think something likes us.”

Olola cocked an eye.

“A talking crow?

“Its amazing!

Olafka blinked.

“Maybe…but what talkative creatures do?”

Olhora thought.

“My thoughts swim deeper than birds.

Olavicola fluttered.

“Would you talk?”

Olhora thought.

“Maybe…

Old Man smiled.

“I talk because I think…

Olhora swooped closer.
